fitScoreNote: Dan is a practitioner-first technical specialist who has already deployed Claude Code in a real enterprise GTM context: he built a 22-skill, 5-agent platform running live on $31.8M in pipeline, not a side project. He is someone who bridges the gap between SE leadership and hands-on AI building, with the credibility of having felt the adoption friction firsthand and solved it from inside a commercial org.

coreJobFunction.rationale: Dan's career is an exceptionally close functional match. He has 3+ years in exactly the customer-facing technical motion the JD describes — Solutions Architect, Customer Engineer, Director of Sales Engineering — with documented evidence of enterprise discovery workshops, custom demo engineering, and driving AI adoption across non-technical users via Claude's Cowork platform. His superpower of translating complex AI systems to non-technical stakeholders is explicitly the reason he survived an 85% RIF and was promoted to VP of Solutions.

seniority.rationale: Dan's profile is a near-exact match for this role's seniority band (3-7+ years customer-facing). He brings ~8 years of post-college experience with a direct progression through Solutions Architect → Customer Engineer → Lead TPM → Director of Sales Engineering, owning $31.8M in enterprise pipeline end-to-end. He is an active, production-level Claude Code power user who built a 22-skill, 5-agent agentic platform, directly satisfying the 'active hands-on experience building with Claude Code' requirement.

technicalSkills.rationale: Dan has extensive, production-level evidence for nearly every technical requirement. He built a production Claude Code agentic platform with 23 specialized skills, 5 parallel subagents, pre/post tool-use hooks, and Cowork deployment to non-technical users. He also built a production hybrid-RAG system with OpenAI embeddings and pgvector, has deep API integration experience, and has held Solutions Architect and Director of SE roles with documented enterprise technical evaluations.

industryVertical.rationale: The Anthropic Claude Code role targets the AI developer tools vertical — companies selling to engineering organizations and AI Champions across industries. Dan's career is concentrated in healthcare AI automation with earlier work in fintech and political tech. However, he has direct production-scale experience operating within the exact vertical this role serves: he deployed Claude's Cowork platform to non-technical teammates and led internal governance for Claude for Enterprise.

logistics.rationale: Dan has lived in San Francisco since January 2020 with deep personal ties to the city, making the SF-based hybrid requirement a zero-friction match. The 25% in-office minimum aligns directly with his stated preference for hybrid. The $180K–$365K compensation band comfortably spans senior IC and director-level SF market rates with no friction.

96% proposal conversion rate as forward-deployed pre-sales technical owner at Thoughtful AI
Only non-Product/Engineering person kept after 85% RIF: retained specifically because I can make complex AI systems legible to the executives who need to trust them
6 months designing MCP-native clinical data platform architecture for enterprise AI adoption

strengths.title: Production Claude Code platform deployed at enterprise scale
strengths.description: Built a 22-skill, 5-agent Cowork platform with pre/post tool-use hooks and org-level governance, running live on $31.8M in enterprise pipeline. Most applicants will have used Claude Code; almost none will have deployed it at this scale with a real feedback loop.

strengths.title: Pre-sales technical authority with 96% proposal conversion
strengths.description: At Thoughtful AI, I became the person placed on calls specifically because of my ability to make complex AI systems legible to enterprise buyers. 96% of accounts I was placed on moved forward. That adoption instinct is the core job in this role.

strengths.title: Field-to-engineering feedback loop (automated, not manual)
strengths.description: Built an agent that populated a market feedback section in Notion every time the engineering team's weekly standup page was created, sourced from that week's discovery calls. Engineers didn't have to ask what customers were saying. It showed up in their workflow.

strengths.title: Only non-Product/Engineering person retained after 85% reduction
strengths.description: When Smarter Technologies shut down, I was one of 15% retained, the only person without a Product or Engineering title. The PE firm made a specific exception. The explicit reason: I have an uncommon ability to take genuinely complex AI systems and make them legible to skeptical, non-technical executives in ways that move them from unsure to convinced. That skill is not adjacent to this role. It is the role.

strengths.title: MCP-native platform design for enterprise AI adoption
strengths.description: Spent six months designing an MCP-native clinical data platform. The reason MCP matters for enterprise Claude Code adoption is the same reason it matters in healthcare: it makes the boundary between the agent and external systems explicit and auditable, something enterprise IT teams can evaluate and approve.

weaknesses.title: No formal external hackathon experience
weaknesses.description: Have designed internal build sprints and enablement rollouts that share the same DNA: scoped deliverables, time-boxed execution, defined success criteria, documented playbooks. No external hackathon credits yet.
weaknesses.mitigation: The facilitation and preparation muscles are there. I would build the hackathon format quickly and I am genuinely excited to.

weaknesses.title: Developer conference talks or keynotes
weaknesses.description: Technical talks have been to enterprise buyers and C-suite, not developer conferences. The technical depth is the same; the venue scale is new.
weaknesses.mitigation: Live architecture walkthroughs with CIOs and security deep-dives with IT leadership require the same preparation discipline as conference talks. Ready for the larger venue.

weaknesses.title: Headless mode and specific Claude Code API surface
weaknesses.description: Production work was embedded in GTM workflows rather than isolated Claude Code API exploration. Pre/post hooks and parallel agent pipelines are direct evidence of the underlying capability.
weaknesses.mitigation: Ramping on headless mode specifics is a matter of days given the production foundation already in place.

referralBlurb: I am referring Dan Mathieson for the Technical Specialist, Claude Code role. Dan spent two years building Claude Code into the operating system of a sales engineering team: 22 skills, 5 parallel agents, pre/post tool-use hooks, a feedback loop sampling real usage, and org-wide Cowork deployment to non-technical reps. He ran technical evaluations with a 96% proposal conversion rate, owned $31.8M in enterprise pipeline, and was retained through an 85% headcount reduction specifically because he can make complex AI legible to people who are not technical. His enablement work has been GTM-internal rather than customer-facing on behalf of a vendor, but the underlying Claude Code depth and enterprise adoption instinct are among the most genuine I have encountered.

projects.name: Personal Website Agent
projects.relevance: The agent panel on this site is built on the Anthropic SDK: tool use, streaming, and a full RAG pipeline grounded in career content. It is the kind of Claude Code-native system this role would help enterprise customers build.

projects.name: Dental Eligibility Intelligence
projects.relevance: Production hybrid-RAG system built for a live healthcare billing workflow, demonstrating the AI system design skills this role requires at the enterprise level.

projects.name: AI Meeting Intelligence
projects.relevance: Built a Claude Code-based multi-agent pipeline that turns meeting recordings into structured deal intelligence in Notion. Shows a prototype version of real-world use cases for Claude Code outside of code generation.

applicationQuestions.question: Why do you want to work at Anthropic? (We value this response highly - great answers are often 200-400 words.)
applicationQuestions.answer:
With this now being the fourth time I've applied to Anthropic in the last four years, the reason I persist in wanting to join the mission remains the same. Namely, I believe Anthropic's mission of safe and reliable AI deployment throughout the economy is of the utmost importance, and that importance continues to grow with each successive application of mine. Dario said it best across multiple media appearances, the best way to ensure safe AI is to lead from the front and give the rest of the space no option but to follow. Claude Code is a direct manifestation of that thesis coming to life, and I'd love to help share the benefits that Claude Code has had on my life, both in my career and personal life, with people across the country and all over the world.

In my work with Claude Code, I'm fascinated most not by the times the technology continues to amaze me, but by the ways I can build tools and workflows that amaze others. Even after Thoughtful AI shut down, I still get messages from my coworkers asking to help them build out Claude Code-based personal knowledge systems for themselves. It's these moments of wonder that continue to prove that leading from the front is the right way to achieve the mission. I know I can help achieve the mission as a technical specialist helping even more people leverage this technology for the benefit of all humanity.
